title: cannotCalculateChanges fallback in chat_delta_sync is correct and must be preserved
description: chat_delta_sync matches ClientError::MethodError { ref error_type, .. } where error_type == 'cannotCalculateChanges' and delegates to a full load_chats reload. This is required by RFC 8620 §5.6: when a server's change history has expired it returns cannotCalculateChanges and the client must fall back to a full /get. The has_more_changes path also falls back correctly. A future contributor might be tempted to remove the fallback (treating it as an edge case or dead code), or to propagate the error upward as a user-visible failure. Neither is correct: cannotCalculateChanges is a normal operational event on long-running connections, not an error condition, and silently falling back to a full reload is the spec-mandated recovery.
close_reason: Already implemented: cannotCalculateChanges correctly falls back to load_chats in chat_delta_sync
